name: YNX DEX

minOS.web: modern browser with ES2022 and PWA support

recoveredCandidate.scope: constant-product contracts, indexer, JavaScript SDK, and Web/PWA preview

strategyVault.scope: immutable owner/engine vault, typed swap and liquidity methods, mandate limits, oracle/depeg checks, pause/revoke/kill, emergency exit, no fee charging

executionAdapter.scope: typed Vault requests, exact Wallet approval digest, injected engine transport, direct/indexed reconciliation, fail-closed gas/fee/oracle/risk snapshots, explicit CPMM collect capability, bounded compound and stepwise rebalance

vaultIndexer.scope: confirmed ActionExecuted ingestion, Vault/FairFlow/LP-Protection-bound cursor v4, authenticated v1/v2/v3 state migration, shared reorg recovery, source-labelled API

fairFlow.scope: on-chain Intent lifecycle, uniform clearing price, solver-funded rebate, commit/reveal competition, verified surplus score, CoW net settlement, exact external inventory, cancellation abort, native bond/reputation, objective slashing, all-stage confirmed indexing and exact canonical Wallet-approved SDK Intent requests
fairFlow.evidence: npm run dex:fairflow:test; npm test --prefix sdk/dex; go test -race ./internal/dex ./cmd/ynx-dex-indexerd

lpProtection.scope: separate protected CPMM Factory, immutable Oracle policy, bounded component fees, depeg swap circuit breaker, delayed configuration, permissionless LP exit, fixed confirmed event indexing/API and exact source-labelled SDK quote/reconciliation
lpProtection.evidence: npm run dex:lp-protection:test; npm test --prefix sdk/dex; go test -race ./internal/dex ./cmd/ynx-dex-indexerd

knownLimitations: The final DEX objective is not implemented or fully tested locally; recovered 0.1 candidate coverage is recorded separately.
No YNX Testnet protocol deployment, verified bytecode, pools, swaps, or LP proofs exist yet.

FairFlow is a local unaudited candidate: UI, Sybil and collusion controls, partial fills, external-route proof and cross-chain settlement are absent; Indexer and SDK paths are local-only and have no public chain evidence.

LP Protection is a local unaudited protected-CPMM candidate: UI presentation, reviewed live Oracle adapter, LVR-aware auction, inventory/range guidance and public chain evidence are absent; Indexer and SDK paths are local-only.
Canonical Wallet verifier and DEX registry entry are not integrated into main.
No independent third-party smart-contract audit has been completed.
CPMM LP fees are embedded in reserves; there is no separate LP collect method, and compound/rebalance require caller-supplied amounts plus sequential Wallet approvals.
Staging, public deployment, hosted download, production signing, and store release are absent.

The Web/PWA prepares deterministic swap and LP reviews, but all transaction authorization remains unavailable until canonical Wallet registry integration and verified Testnet contract addresses exist.
